The superintendent of a vernacular office; chief record-keeper and court-reader. sarishte-dārī , n. f. The office of a sarishtahdār , q. v. sarishtā-i-adālat kī rāh se . In due form; regularly. be-sarishtah, gair-sarishta , adj. 1. Extra judicial ( be-dastūr , 2). 2. Unusual; anomalous. 3. Incoherent; unconnected. be-sarishta , adv. Contrary to rule or practice. be-sarishtagī , n. f. Irregularity; defect of form; informality.

ety: sār˚ = Prk. सार(इ) or सारे(इ) = S. सारय(ति), caus. of rt. सृ
def: v.t. To move aside or away, to remove ( = sarkānā); — to make an end of, consume, expend, exhaust; — to make, perform, achieve, accomplish, effect; to complete, perfect (syn. nibāhnā and sādhnā); to array, to marshal; to tame; to mend, renew, repair, restore; to correct, improve, ameliorate; to mind, observe; — to manure; — to paint (the eyes with collyrium), apply (collyrium).
more: v. a. S. साधन 1. To accomplish; finish ( sādhnā , 1). 2. To apply (collyrium to the eyes). 1. Mā! surmā, kājal karā dharā, Mā, maiṅ nahīṅ sārtī, rī merī mā! Wom. song. The antimony, lamp-black both are here. 'No, mother, no! no glossy paint I'll wear'. 2. Kājal sārūṅ das mareṅ bindī lagāūṅ bīs, Kheṅchū māṅg seṅdūr kī uṭh gin pūre tīs! Dohá. Lamp-black applied, ten slain, my spangle slays twice ten again, If I my middle-part red stain, get up, count thirty slain!
v. a. To perform, accomplish, effect, complete, make, mend, manure.

def: s.f. = saran, q.v. s.m. Making, mending, &c.
more: v. n. S. सिध् do. 1. To suffice; do. 2. To be performed, accomplished, carried on. 3. To pass one's days; to live. 1. Tujhe bhī to us bin nahīṅ sartā. 2. Baiṭhe baiṭhe nā sare, dil ko lag gayā soch, Sab hī ghaṛāī thak gaī, sama nikal gagā poch . Play. To sit down it's no use at all, my mind too anxious grown, Whate'er I've done hath come to nought, the times with evil sown. bāo sarnā ; Sant. gasī , v. n. To fart.
n. m. 1. A kind of pipe; a clarion; hautboy. 2. The inflated skin ( mashk ) on which rivers are crossed. Panj. sarnā'ī, surnāī , n. m. A piper; clarion-player.

v. n. 1. To be rotten; to rot, decay. 1. Haṭ-ke saṛ! Stand off and rot! (Said to one who stinks). 2. Khujlī meṅ saṛ rahā hai . He is rotting with itch. 2. To ferment. 3. To gangrene; corrupt. saṛ'iyal, saṛīlā, saṛā , adj. 1. Rotten; stinking. Jau saṛīlo pān ueṅ pheṅko, jījī, nek karero deo . Wom. 2. ( saṛiyal ) Dirty; filthy.
s. f. An asylum, shelter, protection.
(perhaps from سر End, or from सरण Going, proceeding) v. n. To be performed, to be carried on, to issue, to be ended.

ety: Prk. सरणअं; S. सरणीयं, rt. सृ
def: v.n. To move on or forward, to advance, proceed; to go on smoothly, suitably, or harmoniously; to get on or along, to pass (one's) days, to live; to be carried on, to be finished, be performed, be accomplished; to come to an end, to be expended, be exhausted, be consumed; to issue; — v.t. To pass (as wind; e.g. bāʼo sarnā).

ety: Prk. सडणअं = S. शदनीयं, rt. शद्
def: v.n. To decay, decompose, become rotten or putrid, to rot, putrefy, to gangrene; to ferment.
more: v. a. 1. To rot. 2. To ferment.

ety: S. सर्व+नाम; or P. sar+nām, q.v.
def: adj. Having a famous name, famous, distinguished, well-known, notorious; — sar-nām karnā, v.t. To render famous or notorious, to proclaim, publish, make public; to distinguish, to magnify.
more: adj. Well-known; famous; distinguished. Lā gurū ke nām se maiṅ bhaṅgī sar-nām, Ṭahal karūṅ sarkār meṅ, yehi hamārā kām . Play. sarnām k. , v. a. To make famous or notorious; to proclaim. 1. Namāziyoṅ se khafā rahe, rindoṅ ke sāth kalām kare, But-khāne, masjid ko toṛ-kar, maë-khāne sarnām kare . Free thinkers. Banārsī Dās. With praying men displeased, with wine-bibbers He converse holds, Temples and mosques He breaks, the public house he e'er upholds. 2. Baṅdhvā! nāoṅ likhāūṅ terā! sarkārī parvāne māṅ, Bad-mānas sarnām karāūṅ to ko sagar jamāne māṅ! Rus. O slave! I'll have a writ 'gainst you, a brand fixed on your name, 'Mong criminals you shall be known, proclaimed through time your shame! sar-nām h. , v. n. To be famous.

def: s.f. (dialec.), corr. of sarasvatī, q.v. s.f. Get out just, mother-in-law! this house is mine. 2. Rūṭho mat, sās! bakhar lījo! mahal, aṭārī, sab tum lījo! Ek ṭūṭī jhupaṛyā hameṅ dījo ! Wom. song. Well, dont be angry, mother-in-law, the whole house take, The brick house and the attic, every room just take, A broken hut just give to me a home to make! 3. Dekhat kī atrī patrī, laṭ chhoṛ laṛī sagrī bakhrī, Un devar kī chuṭyā pakṛī, baṛe jeṭh kī mūṅchh ukhāṛ bhagī, Deorānī, nandī dhar mārīṅ, jab sās kahe 'ghar nāhar āī! ' East song. To look at lean and weak, her hair dishevelled all the house she fights, the younger brother-in-law clutched by his hair, the elder's moustache plucked, the younger's wife and sister-in-law knocked down, the mother-in-law exclaims 'a tiger's in the house!'

vulg. sat-bhāʼo, s.m. Actual existence, the being alive or in existence, entity, reality; the quality of goodness, pure or virtuous disposition; good nature or temper, kindly feeling, amiability; — adv. In reality, in earnest

s.m. A virtuous or filial son; a son who performs all the prescribed rites in honour of his ancestors; — a legitimate son (prob. for satī-putr)
s.m. A good road; good course of life, correct or virtuous conduct; sound or orthodox doctrine
s.m. True or honest man, good or virtuous man, worthy or spirited man
adj. & s.m. Having good fruit; — the pomegranate, or any tree producing good fruit
s.m. Association with the good, the society of good and virtuous persons
s.m. Association with the good; — adj. Associating with the good, good, virtuous, pious
s.m. Excellence acquired by association with the good
or sat-saṅgaṭ, s.f. Good companionship, intercourse or association with the good
s.m. Good disposition, goodness of nature

s.m. Doing good or right, kind or benevolent action, kind treatment, hospitable treatment or reception, hospitality; reverence, respect; care, attention; the act of regarding, rewarding, or distinguishing; a pious act; a religious observance; a festival; a meal; funeral rites, obsequies; worship
(-kā), To treat respectfully; to receive hospitably, to entertain, &c.

s.m. One who practises kindness, or benevolence, or virtue, &c. (see sat-kār); a kind or benevolent man, a hospitable man; a virtuous man, &c.; — one who burns the dead; — s.f. Goodness, virtue, truth, honesty, fidelity
s.m. Incumbent acts of kindness or benevolence; religious duties, pious obligations
part. adj. Done well, acted rightly or properly; treated with respect or kindness or hospitality; respected, revered, honoured; saluted; welcomed, entertained; worshipped, adored
s.m. Good or virtuous act, pious deed; virtuous demeanour, piety, virtue; worship; hospitality; funeral obsequies; expiation (syn. sat-kār)

vulg. sat-kiryā or kiriyā, s.f. Good or virtuous action, doing good, charity; virtue (syn. sat-kār and sat-karm); respectful treatment, salutation, welcome, courtesy, worship, homage, hospitality; funeral or obsequial ceremonies; any purificatory ceremony
vulg. sad-gat, s.f. Good state or condition, good estate; good conduct or behaviour; happy state, felicity, fortune; happiness, blessedness
s.m. A good or holy book
(prop. sad-guru), s.m. Chief or excellent guru, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Having good qualities, good, pure, virtuous, excellent, eminent; — good trait, virtue, excellence
adj. & s.m. One who possesses good knowledge, one who is wise in the best sense
s.m. A true speaker, a truthful man ( = satya-vaktā)

def: contrac. of ساتهہ sāth, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi सात (sāta), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀢𑁆𑀢 (satta), from Sanskrit सप्त (saptá).

def: adj. contrac. of ساٿهہ sāṭh, q.v.
s.f. Union, close attachment or intimacy, liaison; league ( = aṭ-saṭ; cf. saṭṭā-baṭṭā)
subs: s.f. Sticking or striking together, adhering, uniting; — succession of rapid strokes, beating, whipping; — adv. In quick succession; rapidly, quickly.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = saut, q.v. s.f. A co-wife, contemporary wife, rival wife (the first wife is called byāhtā; each wife is saut to the other); a rival; pair, twins (e.g. two mangoes, &c. that grow together)
more: n. m. S. सिटी The ear of Indian corn or wheat ( bauṅdī ).
subs: s.m. A spiteful or malicious person, a rival.

def: s.f. An ascetic's matted or clotted hair ( = jaṭā); the hair collected into a loose braid and twisted forwards upon the forehead; a braid of hair; mane (of a lion, &c.); bristles (of a boar); a crest.
s.m. 1˚ = sāṅṭ; — 2˚ A kind of cement or paste ( = saṭan); — 3˚ A graft; — return, exchange.
s.m. An ascetic's matted or clotted hair ( = jaṭā; cf. saṭā).
adj. A hundred
more: adj. A hundred (H. sau ). shat-bhikā , n. m. The twenty-fifth nakshatr or lunar mansion containing one hundred stars, the chief of which is Aquarii.
subs: s.f. lit. 'Requiring a hundred physicians'; name of the twenty-fifth of the Nakshatras or lunar mansions containing 100 stars, the chief of which is λ Aquarii
s.m. The lord or chief of a hundred men, a centurion, a military officer
or śat-padī. s.f. lit. 'Having a hundred feet'; a centipede; the iulus
s.f. The rough tripe of sheep, &c. (syn. ćār-ḵẖāna)
s.f. lit. 'Flowing in a hundred' (branches); the river Satlaj in the Panjāb
s.m. lit. 'Having a hundred bows'; name of a person slain by Krishṇ for having killed Satrājit
adj. & s.m. A hundred thousand; — a lac
adv. By hundreds, in hundreds, a hundred-fold
adj. A hundred times over, a hundred-fold
s.m. A hundred lacs, ten millions
s.m. or śat-mūlī, s.f. lit. 'Having a hundred roots'; the plant Asparagus racemosus
s.m. A hundred years, a century
s.m. lit. 'Piercing a hundred'; a sort of dock or sorrel, Oxalis monadelpha, or Rumex vesicarius.

def: s.m. A petticoat, a gown; a woman's garment; a garment, cloth.
n. m. 1. Return; exchange. 2. A graft. Sāṭe kī sagāī aur biyājū rupaë kā ehsān kyā? Prov. A daughter for a son; alliance made, the interest paid, no obligation on you laid.
part. (f. -ī), Stuck, adhered, &c.
subs: part. adj. (f. -ī), Stuck together, joined, in close contact; attached (to, -se), in league (with); stuck in, inlaid, set, studded.

def: s.m. A species of metre.
s.m. An aggregate of a hundred (see saikṛā).
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit शतक (śataka).

def: n. f. 1. ( saṭkā ) A thin stick. 2. An elastic rod, thick at one end and thin at the other. 3. A small snake. 4. A tube; a hukkā snake.
s.m. A petticoat, a gown; a woman's garment; a garment, cloth.
s.f. A flexible tapering rod; a thin stick, a switch, cane; — a small snake; a tube, a huqqa-snake.
more: s. f. An elastick rod, thick at one end and thin at the other.
